svg.title: AI governance architecture overview
svg.desc: Three user entry points (developers, business workers, customer agents) converge on an AI gateway, which fans out to five security and governance concerns before reaching models and data.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the developer entry point for AI — what tools, risks, and controls apply?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the business worker entry point for AI — what tools, risks, and controls apply?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about customer-facing AI agents — what risks and controls apply?')
sendPrompt('What does an AI gateway or firewall actually do? What products exist for this?')
g.text: All inbound and outbound traffic passes through here — this is not optional

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain prompt injection risks in enterprise AI — how does a gateway defend against it?')
sendPrompt('What are AI output guardrails? How do they prevent harmful or off-brand responses — especially for customer-facing agents?')
sendPrompt('How does data loss prevention work in an AI context? What kinds of leakage are we trying to stop?')
sendPrompt('What does an AI audit trail need to capture? What is the difference between logging for forensics vs. prevention?')
sendPrompt('What does model governance look like — version pinning, approved model lists, system prompt registries?')
sendPrompt('How should data connectors be classified and controlled in an AI rollout — what tiers matter?')
sendPrompt('What does the identity and governance layer need to include for an enterprise AI rollout?')
g.text: SSO, RBAC, policy, training, audit ownership
